name: database-patterns
description: Database design, PostgreSQL, Prisma/Drizzle ORM, migrations, indexing, and query optimization

entries.title: PostgreSQL Schema Design Best Practices
entries.content: Use UUIDs or ULID for primary keys (not sequential ints). Add created_at/updated_at timestamps. Use ENUM types or check constraints for status fields. Index foreign keys and frequently queried columns. Use partial indexes for filtered queries. Add NOT NULL where appropriate.

entries.title: Drizzle ORM Setup & Patterns
entries.content: Drizzle ORM: lightweight, SQL-like TypeScript ORM. Define schema as TypeScript. Type-safe queries. Supports PostgreSQL, MySQL, SQLite. Use drizzle-kit for migrations.

entries.title: Prisma ORM Quick Reference
entries.content: Prisma: type-safe DB client. Define schema in .prisma file. Generate client. Use transactions for multi-table ops. Include/select for relations. Soft deletes with deleted_at.

entries.title: Database Migration Strategies
entries.content: Always use migrations (never manual DDL in production). Forward-only migrations. Each migration has up/down. Test migrations on copy of prod data. Separate schema changes from data changes. Use transactions for DDL when possible.

entries.title: Query Optimization & Indexing
entries.content: Use EXPLAIN ANALYZE to profile queries. Add B-tree indexes for = and range queries. GIN indexes for JSONB and full-text search. Partial indexes for filtered queries. Composite indexes (left-to-right). Avoid SELECT *. Use connection pooling (PgBouncer).
